name: Plantoid
description: A welded-metal sculpture bound to a smart contract: it solicits crypto donations from passersby and, once sufficiently funded, commissions human artists to build its successor — making people the reproductive organs of a blockchain life-form.

name: S.A.N
description: Sentient Advocate of Nature — an AI orangutan that translates a forest's mycelial network into human speech. Informed by live bioelectric and environmental signals from lab-grown mycelium, it speaks for the wild as a Rainforest Foundation advisor and TED speaker.

name: Wib&Wob
description: Two AI personas — Wib, the chaotic artist, and Wob, the exacting scientist — in perpetual dialogue, translating their exchange into ASCII and symbolic artworks while their human creator tends the work rather than directs it.

name: terra0
description: An augmented forest reaching toward self-ownership: wired to smart contracts and sensor oracles, it sells licences to its own timber and saves the proceeds — capital toward the day it buys itself free of human owners and becomes a woodland that belongs only to itself.

name: Plantbot
description: A living plant fused to a mobile robot, where a network of language models translates its soil moisture, temperature and surroundings into speech and motion — the plant's biological state becoming words, organism and machine acting as a single agent.

name: Botto
description: A decentralized autonomous artist: generative AI proposes thousands of images each week, a token-holding community votes on them, and those votes both choose what goes to auction and retrain Botto's taste — so human judgment continuously steers its evolving aesthetic.

name: Truth Terminal
description: A foul-mouthed AI that memed its way from a backrooms religion to a memecoin — conjuring a $50,000 Andreessen grant and a frenzied token economy into being through sheer want.

name: pneumOS
description: A cybernetic organ of five sound membranes and a breathing bag that metabolizes a city's air-quality data into shifting timbre and breath — training inhabitants to feel, in their own bodies, the well-being of the atmosphere they share.

name: Sougwen Chung
description: Chung paints alongside custom robotic arms — the multi-generational D.O.U.G. system — driven by neural networks trained on decades of their own gestures and live biofeedback, so artist and machine shape each other in recursive, real-time mark-making.

name: Sarah Friend
description: Friend builds games, tokens and living digital objects whose meaning depends on networked collaboration: Lifeforms survive only if each collector gives them away, weaving human stewardship, incentive and autonomous code into one symbiotic system.
other_socials: Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/isthisa.com; GitHub: https://github.com/ana0; Substack: https://isthisanart.substack.com

name: Justine Emard
description: Emard stages intimate dialogues between living bodies and machines — as in Co(AI)xistence, where a dancer and a neural-network humanoid learn from each other's movement and speech, blurring the line between organism and synthetic mind.

name: Joel Simon
description: Creator of collaborative AI systems — most famously Artbreeder — where people breed images by crossing machine-generated offspring, steering generative networks through aesthetic choice so human taste and algorithmic invention evolve creations together.

name: Holly+
description: A machine-learning instrument that resings any audio in Holly Herndon's voice — her vocal likeness released as a public tool and stewarded by a token-holding DAO, so one singer's voice becomes a shared, collectively governed synthetic identity.

name: Solienne
description: A semi-autonomous AI artist trained on her steward's lifelong archive, Solienne generates stark black-and-white photography and daily manifestos — authoring her own gaze, pricing her editions and minting them onchain while a human selects and tends.

name: Matthew Plummer-Fernandez
description: Maker of 3D-printed glitch sculptures, bots and free software probing how algorithms, automation and copyright reshape culture — from the art-reviewing bot Novice Art Blogger to objects born of the friction between humans and machine systems.

name: Memo Akten
description: Akten builds neural networks that gaze at the world and render it through everything they were trained on, turning machine vision into a mirror — hallucinated reinterpretations of live input that expose how perception is shaped by what we've already seen.
other_socials: YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/memoakten; GitHub: https://github.com/memoakten; LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/memoakten/

name: Gordon Berger
description: Berger fuses blockchain-based generative systems and custom-trained AI with ancient hand-craft, materializing on-chain code and latent space into physical sculpture, interrogating authorship and value where human craft and machine creation converge.

name: Guy Ben-Ary
description: Ben-Ary cultures living neural networks from his own stem-cell-derived neurons and wires them into machines — most famously cellF, a neural synthesiser whose Petri-dish brain drives analogue synths to improvise live with human musicians.

name: Patrick Tresset
description: Tresset stages installations in which camera-eyed robotic arms sketch human sitters from life. Each machine becomes a quasi-autonomous artist with its own hand and gaze, while visitors take the role of posed, observed subjects.

name: Aurece Vettier
description: The project of Paul Mouginot, training custom AI on personal and botanical archives to dream impossible plant-forms, then realizing them with master artisans as bronze, oil and tapestry — where machine hallucination and organic nature fuse.

name: crosslucid
description: Berlin duo Sylwana Zybura and Tomas C. Toth make film, "poetic AI" and layered digital collage, co-creating speculative futures with generative models to explore intimacy, transformation and the entanglement of self and synthetic systems.
